title: Is house, car, and life insurance permitted in Islam?
summary: Car and health insurance are permitted due to necessity in Western countries. House and property insurance are also allowed. Life insurance remains impermissible unless provided automatically by an employer.

title: Are stock options halal in Islam?
summary: Stock options are haram (impermissible) according to the vast majority of Islamic finance bodies including the OIC Fiqh Academy, European Fatwa Council, and AAOIFI, due to excessive gharar (uncertainty) and qimar (gambling).

title: Is it permissible to short sell stocks in Islam?
summary: Short selling stocks is not permissible according to the vast majority of Islamic finance councils, primarily because you are selling what you do not own, which contradicts the Prophet's explicit hadith.

title: Is zakat due on a woman's gold jewelry?
summary: The safer position is that zakat is due on women's gold jewelry at 2.5%, even though the majority of scholars say it is not required on gold used for personal adornment. The Hanafi position, which requires zakat, has strong evidence since gold can function as currency.

title: How do you calculate zakat on long-term investments like 401k and mutual funds?
summary: For long-term investments not actively traded, zakat is due on one-third of the total value (2.5% of one-third). For day traders actively buying and selling, zakat is due on the full 100%.

title: Can you combine the six fasts of Shawwal with making up missed Ramadan fasts?
summary: You cannot combine the six fasts of Shawwal with makeup Ramadan fasts under a dual intention. You may fast Mondays/Thursdays for makeup and get the sunnah reward. It is better to finish Ramadan fasts before Shawwal fasts, but not required.

title: Can you wipe over regular (non-leather) socks during wudu?
summary: It is permissible to wipe over regular cloth socks during wudu; they do not need to be leather. The conditions are that socks must cover the ankles and you must have had wudu before putting them on.

title: What should you do about halal meat standards when visiting someone else's home?
summary: If you follow a stricter halal meat opinion, you may politely ask about your host's standards without offending them. Participate in the gathering and eat vegetarian if needed. Stunning is makruh but does not make the animal haram.

title: Is vanilla extract permissible in food despite containing alcohol?
summary: Vanilla extract in food is permissible. No food product flavored with vanilla can cause intoxication, so it does not fall under the prophetic prohibition. The same applies to nutmeg and similar extracts.

title: Is obedience to parents unconditionally obligatory in Islam?
summary: Islam commands good conduct toward parents, not unconditional obedience. Obedience is obligatory only when the command is Islamically good, directly benefits the parents, and does not cause disproportionate hardship. You must never be rude regardless.

title: Is it permissible to drink water while standing?
summary: It is better to sit when drinking water, but drinking while standing is permissible. Ibn Abbas reported seeing the Prophet drink while standing. When circumstances make standing natural, there is no issue.
